scenes.text: JavaScript functions are one of the fundamental building blocks of the language. They allow us to encapsulate code into reusable blocks, making our programs more modular and maintainable. Functions are first-class citizens in JavaScript, meaning they can be assigned to variables, passed as arguments to other functions, and returned from functions.

There are different ways to define functions in JavaScript, including function declarations, function expressions, and arrow functions. Each syntax has its own use cases and benefits. Function declarations are hoisted to the top of their scope, allowing them to be called before being defined, whereas function expressions are not hoisted.

Understanding the scope and closures is vital for advanced function usage in JavaScript. Functions create their own scope, and inner functions have access to the outer function's scope, creating closures. Closures enable powerful patterns such as currying, memoization, and encapsulation in JavaScript applications.
